_note: SKILL 1 MEASURE output. First-ever snapshot (the loop had no fresh data to snapshot from 2026-06-01 until the rank-watch pipeline was restored 2026-07-13). Two independent GSC surfaces feed this: (A) per-query rank monitor -> automation/.state/rank-history.json, written by net.jessetek.weekly-rank-watch via get-gsc-rank.mjs; (B) aggregate harvest -> automation/.state/gsc-harvest-*.json, written by the SEPARATE weekly-gsc-harvest scheduled task, source of goal.json baseline_kpis (total impressions/clicks). They can be alive/dark independently.

reading_2026-07-13.honest_interpretation: DO NOT read these zeros as a real reach signal. Even the branded query 'realtor jesse onate' (which the 2026-06-01 baseline had at avg position ~2.5) reads 0 impressions/null rank here. When the branded term also zeroes out, the near-certain cause is a GSC data-finalization artifact: GSC has ~2-3 day latency, so a per-query pull run 2026-07-13 against a too-recent / empty window returns all zeros rather than true 'no visibility'. Treat this reading as PIPELINE-ALIVE-BUT-WINDOW-EMPTY, not as a rank/reach measurement. The next Monday run (querying a settled window) should return real numbers; if branded is STILL 0 then, escalate as a genuine indexing problem.
